# cognitive-science

Cognition emerges not from representation but from enaction—the interface itself is the locus where cognition happens, not merely a shaper of it. Self-reflection across episodes and cataleptic perception (piercing habitual patterns) demonstrate how interface changes fundamentally restructure behavior and awareness. True learning requires crystallizing abstract concepts into actionable structure; knowledge remains liquid until externalized into code, decision patterns, or operational systems.
